mnemonics: NAIVE BAYES: Naturally Assumes Independent Variables Everywhere, Because All Your Events are Separate
keyPoints: Probability-based learning uses statistical methods to predict the likelihood of outcomes.
Bayesian methods update the probability of a hypothesis as more evidence becomes available.
A fundamental assumption in models like Naive Bayes is that features are independent of each other given the class label.
commonMistakes: Confusing prior probability with posterior probability.
Forgetting the 'naive' assumption of feature independence in Naive Bayes, which is often violated in real-world data.
Misapplying Bayes' Theorem by using dependent probabilities without accounting for their dependence.
